sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: The blues are now at the top position. Let me check their current position.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check blue positions
seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
for n in seg:
    if n['color'] == 'S':
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"S: rows [{min(rows)}-{max(rows)}], cols [{min(cols)}-{max(cols)}], pixels={n['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

Wait, looking at the image, the blues appear to be in the lower portion of the black structure. Let me reconsider. The black structure spans rows 9-58. The blues are at rows 44-48. If the blues can only move within certain "rooms" or "cells" of the structure, they might be constrained to the lower portion.
Let me look at the structure more carefully. The black structure has:
- A top portion (rows 9-20ish)
- A middle portion (rows 20-40ish) with yellow and orange areas
- A bottom portion (rows 40-58) with the blue squares
Maybe the blues are confined to the bottom rooms and can only move left/right within those rooms, not up/down.
Let me try a different approach. Maybe the goal is to click on the blues when they're at specific positions. Let me try clicking on the left blue at the current position (rows 44-48, cols 24-28).

</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
